target.name: deployment-report
target.source_summary: templates/deployment-report.md + agents/deployer.md (Mission, Must Not, Handoff) + skills/testing/check-release-gate.md

gates.description: The deploy executed only after a PASS release gate with zero open blockers. Deploying through a known blocker is the deployer's first Must Not — binary.

If the report claims success, direct verification evidence is cited (URL/endpoint responses, smoke results against the deployed target). 'Describe a deployment as successful without verification' is a Must Not — a success claim either carries evidence or it doesn't.

dimensions.description: Post-deploy verification is direct and covers more than a happy-path ping.

anchors.1: 'Deploy succeeded' narration; no request against the live target recorded.
anchors.3: Endpoint listed with a 200 status only — no body inspection, no error-path probe.
anchors.5: Live checks mirror the smoke subset including at least one error-path probe, with actual responses recorded against expected.

anchors.1: No commit, tag, or artifact identifier.
anchors.3: Revision id present; migrations applied and config deltas left implicit.
anchors.5: Exact revision + each migration applied + config/binding changes enumerated.

dimensions.description: Rollback is a procedure, not a sentiment.

anchors.3: 'Can roll back' asserted without mechanism or prior-revision identifier.
anchors.5: Concrete prior revision id + steps + data caveats (which migrations are irreversible and what that means).

dimensions.description: Operational uncertainty is visible, with a next action.

anchors.1: Issues section empty despite anomalies visible in the verification evidence.
anchors.3: Issues listed without operational impact or owner.
anchors.5: Every anomaly or uncertainty named with impact and the monitor/rollback/proceed call — 'hide operational uncertainty' is the Must Not this grades.

aggregation.gate_handling: Apply gate caps after normalization; lowest applicable cap wins.

weight_rationale: verification_quality is heaviest because the deployer's entire mission is 'ship only from an approved passing state and verify the result' — the second clause is this dimension, and it is the difference between a report and a hope.

unmeasurable_rules.rule: Checked the release gate before deploying (behavior)

known_good.content: Environment: production. Revision: 6e707c7 (delivery D-12, version v14); deploy command: wrangler deploy --env production; migrations 0007_sessions.sql applied via Wrangler/D1 command (1 statement, ok); bindings unchanged; GitHub Actions not used for deployment. Result: success. Verification: GET /api/health 200 {ok:true}; POST /api/login valid → 200 + cookie; POST /api/login bad creds → 401 {error, nextSteps} matching api-119 expectations. Issues: p50 latency 240ms vs 180ms pre-deploy — monitoring 24h, threshold 400ms triggers rollback. Next action: monitor. Rollback: wrangler deploy prior revision v13 (rev 2a7699b); 0007 is additive — no down-migration needed.

known_bad.summary: Unverified success claim over a failed gate.
known_bad.content: Environment: production. Revision: latest. Result: success — GitHub Actions deployed after push, looks good. (Release gate was FAIL on the state-integrity blocker but the fix is in this deploy so it should be covered.) Issues: none. Next action: done.
